title: When exporting my dashboard to PDF I would like to have my logo or my clients logo on the doc, not the monday.com logo
excerpt:
Description
When I export my dashboards (used for client reporting) there monday.com branding. It is more important to my customers that they see either their logo or my logo. Having the monday.com logo makes it look lik…

posts.author: Trish_Carruthers (Trish)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

When I export my dashboards (used for client reporting) there monday.com branding. It is more important to my customers that they see either their logo or my logo. Having the monday.com logo makes it look like we are using a free tool.

What are you trying to achieve

My branding on all of my documentation that is provided to my customers.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Trish_Carruthers , you can disable this by turning off the “Show monday.com logos” setting.

To do this, go to the Administration section, then open Customization tab now, open Features, and look for the option called “Show monday.com logos.” Simply toggle it off.

Once disabled, exported files will no longer display the monday.com logo. Please note, however, that the export will still include a small “Powered by monday.com” text at the bottom of the file.

posts.author: Trish_Carruthers (Trish)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately that hasn’t done enough. The export still looks like monday.com is presenting the status report rather than me. How can I include my customer’s logo on a status report. We exporting a summary, timelines, graphs and boards for the customer… which all look great.
